reasoning: SPY at 684.50, yesterday's close with no overnight gap, sits in a narrow indecisive range between Active Support 681.59 (2★, -2.91) and Active Resistance 686.13 (2★, +1.63). The ribbon is MIXED—fast/pivot/slow compressed in a 12¢ band with no bullish or bearish stack—indicating seller/buyer confusion. Price location offers no directional asymmetry: the upside target (695.34 Carry) is farther away ($10.84) but the overhead resistance (686.13) is closer (+1.63). The downside target (675.79 Carry) is similarly distant ($8.71) but the support (681.59) is also -2.91. VIX at 18.87 (mid regime) supplies no urgency. Honest assessment: this is a range-day setup. The battle level is 686.13—if it holds on first test, expect chop down toward 681.59; if it breaks, momentum targets 695.34 Carry. Wait for either level to definitively hold or break before committing to a direction.

primary.condition: if SPY holds 686.13 on first test

secondary.condition: if SPY breaks above 686.13 with volume

key_observations: Ribbon MIXED with 12¢ spread: no bullish or bearish stack conviction—indecision signature.
Price exactly at yesterday's close (684.50): no gap continuation or overnight momentum to ride.
Active levels 1.63 above and 2.91 below create symmetric risk/reward—no edge until one breaks.
